{{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[Prince Kusakabe]], [[Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[Prince Osakabe]], and [[Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[Kii Province]] and [[Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ''' "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[Tanka]]: ''' "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘}} ''' Siblings: [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ''' [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ''' [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ''' [[Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

His involvement in the [[Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[Kii Province]] and [[Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ''' "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[Tanka]]: ''' "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘}} ''' Siblings: [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ''' [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ''' [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ''' [[Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

{{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[Prince Kusakabe]], [[Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[Prince Osakabe]], and [[Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[Kii Province]] and [[Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ''' "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[Tanka]]: ''' "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘}} ''' Siblings: [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ''' [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ''' [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ''' [[Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

revision.text:
{{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=draft}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=draft}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[Prince Kusakabe]], [[Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[Prince Osakabe]], and [[Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[Kii Province]] and [[Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ''' "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[Tanka]]: ''' "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=draft}} ''' Siblings: [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ''' [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ''' [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ''' [[Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

revision.text:
{{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=draft}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=draft}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[Prince Kusakabe]], [[Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[Prince Osakabe]], and [[Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[Kii Province]] and [[Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ''' "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[Tanka]]: ''' "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=draft}} ''' Siblings: [[Princess Ōe]], [[Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ''' [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ''' [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ''' [[Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

revision.text:
{{別人|磯城皇子}} {{基礎情報 皇親 | 名 = 川島皇子 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]] | 生誕 = [[斉明天皇]]3年（[[657年]]） | 死没 = | 薨去 = [[持統天皇]]5年[[9月9日 (旧暦)|9月9日]]（[[691年]][[10月6日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 河島皇子 | 諡号 = | 尊号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 位階 = 浄大参 | 父母 = 父：[[天智天皇]]、母：忍海小竜娘[[色夫古娘]] | 兄弟 = [[弘文天皇]]、[[建皇子]]、'''川島皇子'''、[[志貴皇子]]、[[大田皇女]]、[[持統天皇]]、[[御名部皇女]]、[[元明天皇]]、[[山辺皇女]]、[[明日香皇女]]、[[新田部皇女]]、[[大江皇女]]、[[泉皇女]]、[[水主皇女]]、[[天智天皇#系譜|他]] | 妻 = '''[[泊瀬部皇女]]''' | 子 = [[春日王 (川島皇子の子)|春日王]]、[[三室王]]、[[高丘王]] | 特記事項 = }} '''川島皇子'''（かわしまのみこ・かわしまのおうじ）は、[[天智天皇]]の第二皇子<ref name="s">『[[懐風藻]]』</ref>{{Efn|夭折した兄の[[建皇子]]がいるため、実際には第三皇子である。}}。[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位]]は浄大参。[[淡海氏|淡海朝臣]]・[[春原氏|春原朝臣]]などの祖。'''河島皇子'''とも記される。
== 経歴 == 妃が[[天武天皇]]の皇女（[[泊瀬部皇女]]）<ref name=":0" />なので天皇に重んじられ、天武天皇8年（[[679年]]）天武天皇が[[吉野]]に[[行幸]]した際、鵜野讃良皇后（後の[[持統天皇]]）も列席する中、[[草壁皇子]]・[[大津皇子]]・[[高市皇子]]・[[忍壁皇子]]・[[志貴皇子]]と共に天皇の詔に随い逆らうことなき旨と一同結束とを誓う儀「[[吉野の盟約]]」に参加した<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇8年5月6日条</ref>。天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）詔を奉じて忍壁皇子らと共に「[[帝紀]]及び上古諸事」の編纂を命じられ<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇10年3月17日条</ref>、国史編纂の大事業を主宰、記定には筆頭の編纂者として参与した。
天武天皇14年（[[685年]]）[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が施行されると、忍壁皇子とともに浄大参に叙せられており、[[天智天皇|天智]]・天武の諸皇子の中では[[草壁皇子]]（浄広壱）・[[大津皇子]]（浄大弐）・[[高市皇子]]（浄広弐）に次ぐ序列であった<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇14年正月21日条</ref>。[[朱鳥]]元年（[[686年]]）8月に天智・天武の諸皇子に[[封戸]]が与えられた際、やはり忍壁皇子とともに100戸の加増を受けている<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年8月13日条</ref>。
同年9月に天武天皇が[[崩御]]すると、10月に第3子たる大津皇子が謀反を理由に捕えられ自害させられるが、この際に川島皇子が親友であった大津皇子の翻意および謀反計画を皇太后（持統）に密告したと伝えられる<ref name="s" />。しかし、『[[日本書紀]]』のこの事件に関する記事に川島皇子の名がない上に、褒賞を与えられた形跡もないことから、密告は史実ではないとする見方もある<ref>都倉義孝「大津皇子とその周辺」『万葉集講座』第5巻、有精堂、1973年</ref>。
持統天皇5年（[[691年]]）正月に100戸の加封を受け、合計の封戸は500戸となる。これは2000戸の[[太政大臣]]・高市皇子に次いで、浄広弐・[[穂積親王]]や[[右大臣]]・[[多治比島]]と並ぶ戸数であった<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇5年正月13日条</ref>。同年9月9日薨去。最終冠位は浄大参。越智野（現在の[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[高取町]]の北部から[[明日香村]]西部にかけての低丘陵）に葬られた。
== 人物 == 温厚でゆったりした人柄で、度量も広かった。[[大津皇子]]の謀反事件での対応に対しては、朝廷からは忠誠を賞され、友人からは薄情さを批判され、議者らは[[天智天皇]]の皇子との微妙な立場も踏まえ、厚情か薄情かは明らかにしなかったという。ただし『[[懐風藻]]』の作者も、手段を尽くすことなく親友を苦境に陥らせた川島皇子の態度に関して疑問を呈している。<ref name="s" />
『[[万葉集]]』『[[懐風藻]]』に詩歌が残されている。『懐風藻』には[[五言絶句]]の[[漢詩]]作品1首が採られている。『万葉集』には、[[持統天皇]]4年（[[690年]]）の持統天皇の[[紀伊国]]・[[伊勢国]]への[[行幸]]に従駕した際に詠んだ[[和歌]]作品1首が入集しているが、[[山上憶良]]の作かとの注記がある<ref>『万葉集』巻1-34</ref>。なお、この和歌は『[[新古今和歌集]]』にも採録されている。
== 川島皇子に関する和歌作品 == 『万葉集』に[[柿本人麻呂]]の[[挽歌]]が残されている<ref>『万葉集』巻2-194,195</ref>。
*川島皇子の[[殯|殯宮]]の時、柿本朝臣人麿が泊瀬部皇女に献れる歌一首、また[[短歌]] **飛ぶ鳥の 明日香の川の 上つ瀬に 生ふる玉藻は 下つ瀬に 流れ触らふ 玉藻なす か寄りかく寄り 靡かひし つまの命の たたなづく 柔膚すらを 剣刀 身に添へ寝ねば ぬば玉の 夜床も荒るらむ そこ故に 慰めかねて けだしくも 逢ふやと思ほして 玉垂の 越智の大野の 朝露に 玉藻はひづち 夕霧に 衣は濡れて 草枕 旅寝かもする 逢はぬ君故 *[[反歌]]一首 **敷布の袖交へし君玉垂の 越智野に過ぎぬまたも逢はめやも
== 系譜 == *父：[[天智天皇]] *母：忍海色夫古娘（忍海小竜の娘） **同母姉妹：[[大江皇女]]、[[泉皇女]]
*妃：[[泊瀬部皇女]]<ref name=":0">『[[万葉集]]』巻2-194,195の題詞から想定。</ref>（天武天皇の皇女） *生母不詳の子女 **男子：[[春日王 (川島皇子の子)|春日王]]<ref>塩谷香織「志貴皇子系譜の疑問：市原王は志貴皇子の曽孫ではない」『学習院大学国語国文学会誌』第23号、学習院大学国語国文学会、1980年</ref>（? - 699年） **男子：[[三室王]]<ref name="h">鈴木真年『百家系図稿』巻10,天智天皇御流</ref>（? - 745年） **男子：[[高丘王]]<ref name="h" />（? - 749年）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *宇治谷孟『日本書紀 （下） 全現代語訳』講談社学術文庫、1988年 *宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年
{{Wikiquote|川島皇子}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かわしまのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:天智天皇の子女]] [[Category:7世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:7世紀の歌人]] [[Category:657年生]] [[Category:691年没]]

== 川島皇子に関する和歌作品 == 『万葉集』に[[柿本人麻呂]]の[[挽歌]]が残されている<ref>『万葉集』巻2-194,195</ref>。
*川島皇子の[[殯|殯宮]]の時、柿本朝臣人麿が泊瀬部皇女に献れる歌一首、また[[短歌]] **飛ぶ鳥の 明日香の川の 上つ瀬に 生ふる玉藻は 下つ瀬に 流れ触らふ 玉藻なす か寄りかく寄り 靡かひし つまの命の たたなづく 柔膚すらを 剣刀 身に添へ寝ねば ぬば玉の 夜床も荒るらむ そこ故に 慰めかねて けだしくも 逢ふやと思ほして 玉垂の 越智の大野の 朝露に 玉藻はひづち 夕霧に 衣は濡れて 草枕 旅寝かもする 逢はぬ君故 *[[反歌]]一首 **敷布の袖交へし君玉垂の 越智野に過ぎぬまたも逢はめやも
== 系譜 == *父：[[天智天皇]] *母：忍海色夫古娘（忍海小竜の娘） **同母姉妹：[[大江皇女]]、[[泉皇女]]
*妃：[[泊瀬部皇女]]<ref name=":0">『[[万葉集]]』巻2-194,195の題詞から想定。</ref>（天武天皇の皇女） *生母不詳の子女 **男子：[[春日王 (川島皇子の子)|春日王]]<ref>塩谷香織「志貴皇子系譜の疑問：市原王は志貴皇子の曽孫ではない」『学習院大学国語国文学会誌』第23号、学習院大学国語国文学会、1980年</ref>（? - 699年） **男子：[[三室王]]<ref name="h">鈴木真年『百家系図稿』巻10,天智天皇御流</ref>（? - 745年） **男子：[[高丘王]]<ref name="h" />（? - 749年）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *宇治谷孟『日本書紀 （下） 全現代語訳』講談社学術文庫、1988年 *宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年
{{Wikiquote|川島皇子}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:川島皇子]][[zh:川島皇子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0657|death_date={{death date|0691|10|6}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Hatsusebe|qid=Q7244631}}|father={{ill|Tenji|qid=Q314802}}|relatives={{ill|Abe no Miko|qid=Q11658003}}; {{ill|Aga no Hime Miko|qid=Q11658012}}; {{ill|Genmei|qid=Q233224}}; {{ill|Izumi|qid=Q7244656}}; {{ill|Jitō|qid=Q232026}}; {{ill|Kōbun|qid=Q357242}}; {{ill|Minushi|qid=Q7244760}}; {{ill|Niitabe|qid=Q6647029}}; {{ill|Prince Takeru|qid=Q11486685}}; {{ill|Princess Asuka|qid=Q3275328}}; {{ill|Princess Minabe|qid=Q7244758}}; {{ill|Princess Yamanobe|qid=Q3138027}}; {{ill|Princess Ōe|qid=Q1258730}}; {{ill|Princess Ōta|qid=Q603057}}; {{ill|Shiki no miko|qid=Q11072254}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11055223]] Wikidata name: Prince Kawashima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], and [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.
{{Wikiquote|Prince Kawashima}}

'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], and [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], and [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], and [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />

== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

{{ill|Aga no Hime Miko|WD=Q11658012}}; {{ill|Genmei|WD=Q233224}}; {{ill|Izumi|WD=Q7244656}}; {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Kōbun|WD=Q357242}}; {{ill|Minushi|WD=Q7244760}}; {{ill|Niitabe|WD=Q6647029}}; {{ill|Prince Takeru|WD=Q11486685}}; {{ill|Princess Asuka|WD=Q3275328}}; {{ill|Princess Minabe|WD=Q7244758}}; {{ill|Princess Yamanobe|WD=Q3138027}}; {{ill|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730}}; {{ill|Princess Ōta|WD=Q603057}}; {{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11055223]] Wikidata name: Prince Kawashima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], and [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

{{ill|Aga no Hime Miko|WD=Q11658012}}; {{ill|Genmei|WD=Q233224}}; {{ill|Izumi|WD=Q7244656}}; {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Kōbun|WD=Q357242}}; {{ill|Minushi|WD=Q7244760}}; {{ill|Niitabe|WD=Q6647029}}; {{ill|Prince Takeru|WD=Q11486685}}; {{ill|Princess Asuka|WD=Q3275328}}; {{ill|Princess Minabe|WD=Q7244758}}; {{ill|Princess Yamanobe|WD=Q3138027}}; {{ill|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730}}; {{ill|Princess Ōta|WD=Q603057}}; {{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11055223]] Wikidata name: Prince Kawashima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], and [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />

{{ill|Aga no Hime Miko|WD=Q11658012}}; {{ill|Genmei|WD=Q233224}}; {{ill|Izumi|WD=Q7244656}}; {{ill|Jitō|WD=Q232026}}; {{ill|Kōbun|WD=Q357242}}; {{ill|Minushi|WD=Q7244760}}; {{ill|Niitabe|WD=Q6647029}}; {{ill|Prince Takeru|WD=Q11486685}}; {{ill|Princess Asuka|WD=Q3275328}}; {{ill|Princess Minabe|WD=Q7244758}}; {{ill|Princess Yamanobe|WD=Q3138027}}; {{ill|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730}}; {{ill|Princess Ōta|WD=Q603057}}; {{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11055223]] Wikidata name: Prince Kawashima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]], [[Princess Ōta]], [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]], [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title=[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]], [[Prince Takatsu]], [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], and [[:en:Prince Shiki|Prince Shiki]]<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]] (Jōkōichi), [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the [[:en:Prince Ōtsu|Prince Ōtsu]] incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s 4th-year tour to [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] and [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * [[:en:Tanka|Tanka]]: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: [[:en:Princess Ōe|Princess Ōe]], [[:en:Princess Izumi|Princess Izumi]] * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** [[:en:Prince Takaoka|Prince Takaoka]] (?-749)<ref name="h" />

== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe}}, {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}}, [[Prince Takatsu]], {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}}, and {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki}}<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe}} (Jōkōichi), {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}} (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}} incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō}}'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}'s 4th-year tour to {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}} and {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|WD=Q258331|lt=Tanka}}: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi}} * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka}} (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title={{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō}}|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe}}, {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}}, [[Prince Takatsu]], {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}}, and {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki}}<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe}} (Jōkōichi), {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}} (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}} incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō}}'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}'s 4th-year tour to {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}} and {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|WD=Q258331|lt=Tanka}}: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi}} * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka}} (?-749)<ref name="h" />
