{{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254}}}} {{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}, 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki}}, [[Princess Ōta]], {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}, [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi}}, [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka}} | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title={{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō}}|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe}}, {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}}, [[Prince Takatsu]], {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}}, and {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki}}<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe}} (Jōkōichi), {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}} (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu}} incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō}}'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}'s 4th-year tour to {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}} and {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|WD=Q258331|lt=Tanka}}: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi}} * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka}} (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}}, {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}}, [[Prince Takatsu]], {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe|1=Prince Osakabe}}, and {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki|1=Prince Shiki}}<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}} (Jōkōichi), {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}} (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}} incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s 4th-year tour to {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} and {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|WD=Q258331|lt=Tanka|1=Tanka}}: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe|1=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi|1=Princess Izumi}} * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka|1=Prince Takaoka}} (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title={{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}}, {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}}, [[Prince Takatsu]], {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe|1=Prince Osakabe}}, and {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki|1=Prince Shiki}}<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}} (Jōkōichi), {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}} (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}} incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s 4th-year tour to {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} and {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|WD=Q258331|lt=Tanka|1=Tanka}}: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe|1=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi|1=Princess Izumi}} * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka|1=Prince Takaoka}} (?-749)<ref name="h" />

However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s 4th-year tour to {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} and {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|WD=Q258331|lt=Tanka|1=Tanka}}: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe|1=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi|1=Princess Izumi}} * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka|1=Prince Takaoka}} (?-749)<ref name="h" />
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Ujitani, Takeru. ''Nihon Shoki (Lower Volume): Full Modern Translation.'' Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Hoga, Toshio. ''Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies.'' Research Group on Ancient Clans, 1986.

{{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}}} {{Person|磯城皇子}} {{Infobox Imperial Family Member | name = Prince Kawashima | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Asuka period]] | birth_date = 3rd year of [[Empress Saimei]] (657) | death_date = | demise = 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}, 9th day of the 9th month (October 6, 691) | other_names = Prince Kawashima (河島皇子) | posthumous_name = | honorific_title = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = Jōdaisan (浄大参) | parents = Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}; Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海小竜娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = [[Emperor Kōbun]], [[Prince Take]], Prince Kawashima, {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki|1=Prince Shiki}}, [[Princess Ōta]], {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}, [[Princess Minabe]], [[Empress Genmei]], [[Princess Yamabe]], [[Princess Asuka]], [[Princess Nittabe]], {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe|1=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi|1=Princess Izumi}}, [[Princess Mizushi]], among others | spouse = [[Princess Hasebe]] | children = [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]], [[Prince Mimuro]], {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka|1=Prince Takaoka}} | notable_achievements = }}
'''Prince Kawashima''' (川島皇子, also 河島皇子) was the second son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}<ref name="s">*{{cite book|title={{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}|year=8th century}}</ref>. He held the court Rank of Jōdaisan (浄大参) and was the progenitor of clans such as the {{ill|Ōmi no Asomi|ja|淡海朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Harahara no Asomi|ja|春原朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harahara no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == Prince Kawashima was married to [[Princess Hasebe]], the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. In the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (679), he participated in the [[Oath of Yoshino]], a ceremony pledging loyalty to the emperor alongside other imperial princes such as {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}}, {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}}, [[Prince Takatsu]], {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe|1=Prince Osakabe}}, and {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki|1=Prince Shiki}}<ref>''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', entry for the 8th year of Emperor Tenmu, 5th month, 6th day</ref>. In the 10th year (681), Prince Kawashima was ordered to assist in compiling the "Records of the Sovereigns and Various Events of Antiquity" (帝紀及び上古諸事), a monumental task of national historiography<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu, 3rd month, 17th day</ref>.
In the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (685), Prince Kawashima attained the Rank of Jōdaisan. Among the imperial princes, his status was surpassed only by {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|WD=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}} (Jōkōichi), {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}} (Jōdaichi), and [[Prince Takatsu]] (Jōkōni)<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu, 1st month, 21st day</ref>. In Shuchō 1 (686), following Emperor Tenmu's death, Prince Kawashima received an additional 100 households as a stipend, bringing his total to 500 households, a Rank comparable to leading officials of the court<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', entry for the 5th year of Empress Jitō, 1st month, 13th day</ref>. He passed away later that year and was interred at Ochi-no in present-day [[Takaichi District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Legacy == Prince Kawashima is remembered as a warm and magnanimous individual. His involvement in the {{ill|Prince Ōtsu|en|Prince Ōtsu|WD=Q1284081|lt=Prince Ōtsu|1=Prince Ōtsu}} incident has been debated, with some sources suggesting he reported Ōtsu’s alleged rebellion to the court. However, the ''Nihon Shoki'' does not corroborate this, leading scholars to question its veracity<ref>Tokura Yoshitaka, "Prince Ōtsu and His Surroundings," ''Manyōshū Lecture Series'', Vol. 5, Yūseidō, 1973</ref>.
Both the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}'' and the ''[[:en:Manyōshū|Manyōshū]]'' contain works attributed to him, showcasing his talent in poetry and literature. The ''Kaifūsō'' includes one Chinese-style [[wuyan jueju]] poem, while the ''Manyōshū'' features a single Japanese waka attributed to him during {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s 4th-year tour to {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} and {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 1-34</ref>.
== Works == The ''Manyōshū'' contains an elegy by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}} for Prince Kawashima<ref>''Manyōshū'', Vol. 2-194, 195</ref>.
* Elegy presented to Princess Hasebe upon Prince Kawashima's death: ** "Like the seaweed on the u11er reaches of the river Asuka, it sways and intertwines. My lord, whose soft skin I embraced, cannot lie by my side with sword in hand. The night-bed is cold, and I wander alone, wet with morning dew on the fields of Ochi, where we cannot meet again." * {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|WD=Q258331|lt=Tanka|1=Tanka}}: ** "The sleeves of our shared bed lie forgotten in Ochi’s fields. Shall we ever meet again?"
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} * Mother: {{ill|Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja|忍海色夫古娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokami no Shoryu's daughter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Siblings: {{ill|Princess Ōe|en|Princess Ōe|WD=Q1258730|lt=Princess Ōe|1=Princess Ōe}}, {{ill|Princess Izumi|en|Princess Izumi|WD=Q7244656|lt=Princess Izumi|1=Princess Izumi}} * Wife: [[Princess Hasebe]]<ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Manyōshū, Vol. 2}}</ref> * Children: ** [[Prince Kasuga (son of Prince Kawashima)|Prince Kasuga]] (?-699)<ref>Shioya Kaori, "Doubts about the Genealogy of Prince Shiki," ''Gakushūin Journal'', Vol. 23, 1980</ref> ** [[Prince Mimuro]] (?-745)<ref name="h">{{cite book|author=Suzuki Masatoshi|title=Hundred Family Genealogies, Vol. 10}}</ref> ** {{ill|Prince Takaoka|en|Prince Takaoka|WD=Q11669578|lt=Prince Takaoka|1=Prince Takaoka}} (?-749)<ref name="h" />
